title: Plant record
description: One medicinal-plant species and the human editing unit. Actions and indications reference controlled vocabularies; constituents reference compound entities; every claim carries its own references and status.

id.description: Stable slug: lowercase scientific name, spaces -> hyphens, e.g. allium-sativum.

common_slug.description: Public URL slug for /plants/<common_slug>/. Optional OVERRIDE: when omitted, scripts/build.py derives it from the primary common name (collisions fall back to the Latin id). Set explicitly to pin a stable SEO URL and a redirect from the Latin-binomial form.

family.description: Botanical family; must be a real family (never 'Unknown').

external_ids.description: Anchors botanical identity and enables enrichment.

wp_post_id.description: WordPress Materia Medica post id, or null if no page yet.

parts_used.description: Canonical part names, e.g. Bulb, Leaf, Root, Rhizome, Flower.

compounds.description: Compound entity ids -> compounds/<id>.yaml (classes + named bioactives detected in name).

actions.description: Pharmacological actions (mechanism). Each entry is a sourced claim.

parts.description: Plant part(s) this claim applies to. Omit when the source did not specify.

note.description: Original free-text phrasing / mechanism detail, preserved verbatim.

indications.description: What the plant is used FOR (conditions/symptoms). Powers the Symptom-to-Plant Lookup.

evidence_override.description: Optional editorial override of the auto-computed 1-10 evidence score. Omit to use the deterministic study-type + volume score produced by scripts/build.py (Option B).

note.description: Original free-text phrasing / evidence detail, preserved verbatim.

drug_class_interactions.description: Herb <-> drug-CLASS interaction claims (powers the Herb-Drug Interaction Checker, Prompt 1). Each maps this plant to one drug class with a severity tier. Public tools serve status: approved ONLY.

mechanism.description: Plain-English mechanism / reason for the interaction.

reviewed_by.description: Reviewer who approved this record (medical sign-off).

pairings.description: Herb <-> herb synergy / caution pairings (powers the Herb-Herb Combinations tool, Prompt 2). Directionless: a pairing stored on plant A is mirrored to plant B by scripts/build.py. Public tools serve status: approved ONLY.

note.description: Short cited note on the pairing.

dangerous_lookalikes.description: Dangerous plants/fungi this medicinal plant is realistically mistaken for, and how to tell them apart (powers the Dangerous Lookalikes tool). One-directional (NOT mirrored, unlike pairings). Public tools serve status: approved ONLY.

confused_part.description: Which part/growth stage is confusable, e.g. 'young leaves / basal rosette', 'root', 'whole plant before flowering'.

confusion_context.description: Plain-English why/when the mix-up happens (co-occurrence, foraging scenario, documented poisonings).

distinguishing_features.description: Rock-solid, observable ways to tell the MEDICINAL plant apart from the dangerous one. Each must be reliable enough to bet a life on — omit anything subtle or ambiguous.

key_test.description: The single most reliable field check, e.g. 'Crush a leaf: true fennel smells strongly of aniseed; hemlock smells foul/mousy.'

lookalikes_review.description: Record-level completeness flag distinguishing 'researched, none known' from 'not yet researched'. Absent = NOT researched (tool must not render 'safe').

reference_ids.description: For a 'none-known' assertion, the source(s) that support it.

references.description: Background/general sources for the plant not tied to a specific claim (identification, distribution, foraging, overviews).

provenance.description: Origin of the record, e.g. 'book', 'website', 'pubmed', or combinations like 'book+pubmed'.

internal_notes.description: Editorial notes and history; NEVER published.

status.description: Record-level status (usually the lowest-confidence claim governs).

safety_status.description: Safety-record lifecycle. 'draft' = Claude-drafted / under review, NEVER served publicly. 'approved' = human medical sign-off, served by public tools. Deliberately distinct from the content `status` enum.

severity.description: Herb-drug severity tier: avoid | caution (use with caution) | likely-safe | insufficient (insufficient evidence).

lookalike_severity.description: Dangerous-lookalike lethality tier: fatal | dangerous | irritant | caution.
